title: Gridded time series data manipulation and analysis
text: gts provides lightweight workflow working gridded environmental arrays R, especially data stored netCDF files. package centred three complementary object types: gts: gridded time-series objects; static: time-invariant gridded spatial fields bathymetry, shelf break, cell area, distance coast; grid: spatial grid objects storing geometry, masks, associated metadata. package aims make common operations environmental grids easier reproducible inside R: reading writing netCDF files, inspecting metadata, subsetting space time, regridding, filling missing values, computing climatologies summaries, reshaping tabular form, combining point observations gridded products. Full documentation available https://roliveros-ramos.github.io/gts/.

Lower-level constructors also available already arrays metadata memory: gts() build gts object array open netCDF handle; make_grid() create regular grid programmatically.

what: Summarise and analyse the time dimension
title: Gridded time series data manipulation and analysis
code: # time-series summaries sst_t = mean(sst, by = "time") plot(sst_t) sst_s = sd(sst, by = "space") plot(sst_s)  # climatology sst_clim = climatology(sst, FUN = "mean") plot(sst_clim, time=3)  # quantiles over time sst_q = quantile(sst, probs = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9)) plot(sst_q) # quantile 10% SST

title: Gridded time series data manipulation and analysis
text: Read write netCDF-backed gridded data. Work time-varying (gts) time-invariant (static) gridded objects. Store reuse spatial grid metadata grid objects. Use familiar time-series generics time(), frequency(), window(), cycle() gridded time-series objects. Regrid, interpolate, subset, fill environmental grids. Compute climatologies, summaries, quantiles, vertical integrations. Reshape gridded objects tabular form merge point records.
